name: Oklahoma SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
description: Oklahoma's food assistance program providing monthly EBT benefits to purchase groceries. Administered by the Oklahoma Department of Human Services (OKDHS).
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families meeting federal SNAP income guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: Gross income at or below 130% FPL; net income at or below 100% FPL

name: SoonerCare (Oklahoma Medicaid)
description: Oklahoma's Medicaid program providing health coverage for eligible low-income residents. Covers doctor visits, hospital care, prescriptions, mental health, dental, and vision.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income adults, children, pregnant women, elderly, and individuals with disabilities
incomeLimitNotes: Adults up to 138% FPL under Medicaid expansion; children up to 210% FPL

name: Oklahoma Unemployment Insurance
description: Provides temporary wage replacement benefits to workers who lost their jobs through no fault of their own. Administered by the Oklahoma Employment Security Commission.
eligibilitySummary: Workers who lost employment through no fault of their own and meet wage/work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Must have earned sufficient wages during the base period

name: Oklahoma Housing Finance Agency (OHFA)
description: Provides affordable housing resources including rental assistance, homebuyer programs, and housing development for Oklahoma residents.
eligibilitySummary: Low to moderate income individuals and families seeking affordable housing
incomeLimitNotes: Varies by program; generally targets households at or below 80% AMI

name: Legal Aid Services of Oklahoma
description: Provides free civil legal assistance to low-income Oklahomans in matters including housing, family law, consumer issues, and public benefits.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals at or below 125% of the federal poverty level

name: Oklahoma Works (Workforce Development)
description: Oklahoma's workforce development system offering job search assistance, training programs, resume help, and career counseling through centers statewide.
eligibilitySummary: All Oklahoma residents seeking employment or career development services

name: Oklahoma LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income households pay heating and cooling bills. Administered through the Oklahoma Department of Human Services.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income households with home energy costs, prioritizing elderly, disabled, and young children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 150% FPL

name: Oklahoma 211 (Heartline)
description: Free, confidential referral service connecting Oklahomans with local health and human services resources including food, housing, utilities, and crisis support.
eligibilitySummary: All Oklahoma residents seeking assistance or referrals
languages: English, Spanish, and other languages via interpreter

name: Regional Food Bank of Oklahoma
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 53 counties in central and western Oklahoma. Distributes food through a network of 1,300+ partner agencies and programs.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in central and western Oklahoma

name: Community Food Bank of Eastern Oklahoma (Tulsa)
description: Feeding America affiliate serving 24 counties in eastern Oklahoma. Provides food distribution through partner agencies, food pantries, and mobile markets.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in need of food assistance in eastern Oklahoma

name: Oklahoma Department of Veterans Affairs
description: Assists Oklahoma veterans and their families in obtaining federal and state benefits including healthcare, disability compensation, education, and veterans centers.
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, active duty service members, and their dependents residing in Oklahoma

name: Oklahoma Crisis Line (COPES)
description: Statewide crisis line providing immediate support for mental health emergencies, suicidal thoughts, and emotional distress through the ODMHSAS crisis network.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in Oklahoma experiencing a mental health crisis

name: Oklahoma Child Care Subsidy Program
description: Provides financial assistance to help low-income families pay for childcare so parents can work, attend school, or participate in training programs.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income working families with children who need childcare
incomeLimitNotes: Family income at or below 85% SMI

name: Oklahoma TANF (Temporary Assistance for Families)
description: Cash assistance program providing monthly benefits and supportive services to low-income families with children to help achieve self-sufficiency.
eligibilitySummary: Very low-income families with dependent children who meet work requirements
incomeLimitNotes: Very low income thresholds; varies by family size

name: Oklahoma WIC Program
description: Women, Infants, and Children supplemental nutrition program providing healthy foods, nutrition education, and breastfeeding support for eligible participants.
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children up to age 5

name: EMBARK (Oklahoma City Transit)
description: Public transit system serving the Oklahoma City metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service, rapid transit, and paratransit options.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Oklahoma City metro area; reduced fares for seniors and disabled

name: Tulsa Transit
description: Public transit system serving the Tulsa metropolitan area with fixed-route bus service and paratransit options for the greater Tulsa region.
eligibilitySummary: Available to all riders in the Tulsa metro area

name: Oklahoma Weatherization Assistance Program
description: Provides energy efficiency improvements to low-income homes to reduce energy costs. Includes insulation, air sealing, and HVAC system upgrades.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income homeowners and renters, with priority for elderly, disabled, and families with children
incomeLimitNotes: Household income at or below 200% FPL

name: Oklahoma Department of Rehabilitation Services
description: Provides vocational rehabilitation services for Oklahomans with disabilities including job training, placement assistance, and assistive technology.
eligibilitySummary: Individuals with physical or mental disabilities that create barriers to employment

name: Oklahoma City Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Oklahoma City area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Oklahoma City area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Tulsa Housing Authority
description: Provides affordable public housing and Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) for low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Tulsa area.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income families, elderly, and disabled individuals in the Tulsa area
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by family size; generally at or below 80% AMI for admission

name: Oklahoma Domestic Violence Hotline
description: Statewide hotline providing crisis intervention, safety planning, shelter referrals, and support for victims of domestic violence in Oklahoma.
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or affected by domestic violence in Oklahoma

name: Oklahoma's Promise (OHLAP)
description: Scholarship program that pays tuition at Oklahoma public colleges for students who enroll in 8th-10th grade and meet academic and conduct requirements.
eligibilitySummary: Oklahoma students who enroll in 8th-10th grade with family income at or below $60,000
incomeLimitNotes: Family income must be $60,000 or less at time of application

name: Oklahoma Adult Education and Literacy
description: Provides free GED preparation, adult basic education, and English as a Second Language programs at locations across Oklahoma.
eligibilitySummary: Adults who need basic education, GED preparation, or English language instruction

name: Oklahoma Head Start Association
description: Federally funded early childhood education program promoting school readiness for children from birth to age 5 in low-income Oklahoma families.
eligibilitySummary: Families with children birth to age 5 at or below federal poverty guidelines
incomeLimitNotes: 100% FPL or below; foster and homeless children automatically eligible

name: Tulsa Area United Way
description: Provides community programs and funding for education, health, and financial stability services in the greater Tulsa area.
eligibilitySummary: Residents of the greater Tulsa area

name: Community Action Agencies of Oklahoma
description: Network of community action agencies providing comprehensive anti-poverty services including emergency assistance, housing, energy, and workforce development.
eligibilitySummary: Low-income individuals and families throughout Oklahoma

name: SoonerCare Online Application
description: Oklahoma's online portal for applying to Medicaid (SoonerCare) and checking application status. Streamlined process for health coverage enrollment.
eligibilitySummary: All Oklahoma residents can use this portal to apply for SoonerCare

name: Oklahoma Tribal Assistance Programs
description: Various tribal nations in Oklahoma offer assistance programs for enrolled members including food distribution, housing, healthcare, and social services.
eligibilitySummary: Enrolled members of federally recognized tribes in Oklahoma

name: Oklahoma Lifeline Telephone Assistance
description: Provides discounts on monthly telephone or broadband service for qualifying low-income Oklahoma households to maintain access to essential communications.
eligibilitySummary: Households participating in SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, or with income at or below 135% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: 135% FPL or participation in qualifying federal assistance program

name: Oklahoma Legal Aid Online Resources
description: OKLaw.org provides free legal information, forms, and referrals for Oklahomans facing legal issues related to housing, family, employment, and benefits.
eligibilitySummary: All Oklahoma residents seeking legal information; direct services for low-income individuals
